findings.evidence: Lines 72-85 show the existing pattern: `measurable_tailProcess_directingMeasure_coe` has no `hTail`, while `measurable_directingMeasure_coe` is only the ambient corollary; lines 123-128 add only the ambient bundled version and call `measurable_directingMeasure_coe hTail hB`.
findings.fix: First prove `measurable_tailProcess_directingProbabilityMeasure : Measurable[tailProcess X] (directingProbabilityMeasure μ X)` using `measurable_tailProcess_directingMeasure_coe`, then make the current ambient theorem a corollary via `.mono hTail le_rfl`.
findings.issue: `measurable_directingProbabilityMeasure` requires `hTail : tailProcess X ≤ ‹MeasurableSpace Ω›`, but the nearby characteristic theorem is already stated at the stronger natural level `Measurable[tailProcess X]`.

summary: The bundled measurability theorem is stated only in the ambient measurable space, although the existing unbundled API already proves the stronger tail-process measurability. Add the natural tail-process version and derive the current `ConditionallyIIDWith`-shaped corollary from it.
